title: Writing: Constructing Alternate Realities
learningGoal: Write grammatically correct conditional sentences, distinguishing between Real Future conditions and Unreal Present/Past hypotheticals.
instructions:
You must translate three conditional sentences. Pay strict attention to the TENSE. 
Real Future = Use Future Tense.
Hypothetical = Use Root + ta/ti/te (NO hai/tha). 
Assume you are a MALE speaker.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences (MALE speaker):
1. Real Future: If I go to Delhi, then I will meet him. (Go = Jaa. Meet = Mil. Him = Us-se)
2. Unreal Present: If I had money, then I would buy a car. (Money = Paise. Have = Paas hona. Buy = Khareed. Car = Gaadi)
3. Unreal Past (Regret): If you had told me, then I would have come. (Tell = Bata. Me = Mujhe. Come = Aa)
promptGoal: Apply the three core conditional structures.
referenceHints: Sentence 1: 'Agar main Delhi jaunga, toh main us-se milunga.' (Both future)
Sentence 2: 'Agar mere paas paise hote, toh main gaadi khareedta.' (Cutoff verbs)
Sentence 3: 'Agar tumne mujhe bataya hota, toh main aa jaata.' (Past participle + hota)

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: अगर मैं दिल्ली जाऊँगा, तो मैं उससे मिलूँगा। अगर मेरे पास पैसे होते, तो मैं गाड़ी खरीदता। अगर तुमने मुझे बताया होता, तो मैं आ जाता।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Agar main Delhi jaunga, toh main us-se milunga. Agar mere paas paise hote, toh main gaadi khareedta. Agar tumne mujhe bataya hota, toh main aa jaata.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: If I go to Delhi, then I will meet him. If I had money, then I would buy a car. If you had told me, then I would have come.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect mapping of the three conditional forms: Future-Future, Root(ta)-Root(ta), Past(hota)-Root(ta).

evaluationCriteria.description: Sentence 1 must use future tense in both clauses ('jaunga', 'milunga') or past-future ('gaya', 'milunga').
Sentence 2 MUST NOT have 'hain' or 'tha' at the end. It must end abruptly on 'hote' and 'khareedta'.
Sentence 3 must use 'hota' for the condition (bataya hota), and 'ta' for the result (aata/aa jaata).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: अगर मैं दिल्ली जाऊँगा, तो मैं उससे मिलूँगा। अगर मेरे पास पैसे होते, तो मैं गाड़ी खरीदता। अगर तुमने मुझे बताया होता, तो मैं आ जाता।
acceptableAlternates: Agar main Delhi gaya, toh main usse milunga. Agar mere paas paise hote, toh main gaadi khareedta. Agar tumne mujhe bataya hota, toh main aata.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Extremely strict on checking for the absence of 'tha/hai' in sentence 2 and 3.

criteria.name: Hypothetical Failure
criteria.description: If user writes 'Agar mere paas paise hote hain' or 'main gaadi khareedta tha', deduct heavily. Hypotheticals CANNOT have auxiliary verbs.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You have mastered the three tiers of conditional logic in Hindi.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 3 of the lesson. The most common mistake is adding 'tha' (was) at the end of imaginary sentences. Don't do it! Just end on 'ta/te/ti'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Sentence 1 is a real possibility, so we use future tense. Sentences 2 and 3 are imaginary, so we use the special 'ta' cutoff form.

hintText: For sentence 2, literally write 'If with me money were, then I car buy.' Do not add 'am' or 'was' at the end of the sentence.
commonMistakes: Adding 'tha' at the end of 'khareedta', making it 'khareedta tha' (which means 'used to buy' instead of 'would buy').
